memoryArtifacts.description: Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland built in 1163 on Walter FitzAlan's land, 1st steward of Scotland  User:Colin / Wikimedia Commons / CC BY-SA 3.0

memoryArtifacts.headline: Walter's name and title as it appears in a royal charter to Holyrood Abbey: "Walter filio alani Dapifero".

memoryArtifacts.description: Twelfth-century secular lordships on the western seaboard of Scotland. Walter's domain included the depicted regions of Strathgryfe, Renfrew, Mearns, and North Kyle. Clydesdale and South Kyle were royal lordships, whilst Cunningham was a Morville lordship.

memoryArtifacts.headline: Walter FitzAlan's Seal, 1st Steward of Scotland.
memoryArtifacts.description: Walter FitzAlan's Seal, 1st steward of Scotland. The image has been scanned from Descriptive catalogue of impressions from ancient Scottish seals, royal, baronial ecclesiastical, and municipal, embracing a period from a.d. 1094 to the commonwealth, plate 3, figure 1, by Henry Laing, published in 1850. This book was published in Edinburgh, Scotland. The illustration is not credited. The seal reads "SIGILLUM WALTERI FILLI ALANI DAPIFERI REG.".

popularStory.headline: William FitzAlan 1st Lord of Oswestry
popularStory.description: William was a rebel. He defended Shrewsbury Castle against Stephen in 1138, and afterwards joined the army of the Empress Matilda and her son, who restored him his fiefs. His younger brother, Walter, …

zh.lifeSummary: 當William FitzAlan Lord of Oswestry於1105年出生在英國史洛普爾奧斯維斯崔時，他的父親Alan Dapifer FitzFlaad27歲，母親Avelina de Hesdin30歲。他於大約1130年與Christiana結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1161年住在英國瓦維其可爾。他於1138年被列為sheriff of shropshire。他去世於大約1160年3月27日在英國史洛普爾奧斯維斯崔享年55歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭史洛普爾史福斯波利史福斯波利 霍利 克羅斯 煙雨 聖 基路士。

nameMeanings.name: FitzAlan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + the personal name Alan ( see Allen ), ‘son of Alan’.

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Allen
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Alain, Alein (Old Breton Alan), from a Celtic personal name of great antiquity and obscurity. In England the personal name is now usually spelled Alan, the surname Allen; in Scotland the surname is more often Allan. From 1139 it was common in Scotland, where the surname also derives from Gaelic Ailéne, Ailín, from ail ‘rock’. The present-day frequency of the surname Allen in England and Ireland is partly accounted for by the popularity of the personal name among Breton followers of William the Conqueror, by whom it was imported first to Britain and then to Ireland. Saint Alan(us) was a 5th-century bishop of Quimper, who was a cult figure in medieval Brittany. Another Saint Al(l)an was a Cornish or Breton saint of the 6th century, to whom a church in Cornwall is dedicated.
English: occasionally perhaps from the rare Middle English femaje personal name Aline (Old French Adaline, Aaline), a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Adal-, especially Adalheidis (see Allis ).
French: variant of   Allain  , a cognate of 1 above, and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When William FitzAlan Lord of Oswestry was born in 1105, in Oswestry, Shropshire, England, his father, Alan Dapifer FitzFlaad, was 27 and his mother, Avelina de Hesdin, was 30. He married Christiana about 1130. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He lived in Warwickshire, England in 1161. In 1138, at the age of 33, his occupation is listed as sheriff of shropshire. He died about 27 March 1160, in Oswestry, Shropshire, England, at the age of 55, and was buried in Shrewsbury Holy Cross with St Giles, Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Christiana environ 1130. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il a vécu à Warwickshire, Angleterre en 1161. En 1138, à l’âge de 33 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est sheriff of shropshire. Il est décédé environ 27 mars 1160, à Oswestry, Shropshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterré à Shrewsbury Holy Cross with St Giles, Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Er hat um 1130 Christiana geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. 1161 hat er in Warwickshire, England gelebt. 1138, als er 33 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als sheriff of shropshire aufgeführt. Er starb um 27. März 1160 im Alter von 55 Jahren in Oswestry, Shropshire, England und wurde in Shrewsbury Holy Cross with St Giles, Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ko.lifeSummary: William FitzAlan Lord of Oswestry(이)가 1105년 영국 슈럽실르 오스베스트리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Alan Dapifer FitzFlaad(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Avelina de Hesdin(은)는 30세였다. 그는 대략 1130년 Christiana(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1161년도에 영국 워윅실르에서 살았다. 1138년, 33세의 나이로 그의 직업은 sheriff of shropshire이었다. 그는 대략 1160년 3월 27일 영국 슈럽실르 오스베스트리에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 슈레w스버이 슈레w스버이 홀이 크로스 위스 스트 자일스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William FitzAlan Lord of Oswestry nasceu em 1105, em Oswestry, Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Alan Dapifer FitzFlaad, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Avelina de Hesdin, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Christiana aproximadamente 1130. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele viveu em Warwickshire, Inglaterra em 1161. Em 1138, com 33 anos, sua profissão aparece como sheriff of shropshire. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 27 de março de 1160, em Oswestry, Shropshire, Inglaterra, com 55 anos, e foi sepultado em Shrewsbury Holy Cross with St Giles, Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William FitzAlan Lord of Oswestry родился в 1105 г. в местности освестри, шропшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Alan Dapifer FitzFlaad было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Avelina de Hesdin, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Christiana около 1130 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он жил в местности варвиккшир, Англия в 1161 году. В 1138 году, в возрасте 33 лет,  он работал по профессии sheriff of shropshire. Он умер около 27 марта 1160 г. в местности освестри, шропшир, Англия в возрасте 55 лет и был похоронен в местности шрюсбури холи кросс вит ст джилесь, шрюсбури, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания.
